revision.text:
'''奈良豆比古神社の翁舞'''（ならずひこじんじゃのおきなまい）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]奈良坂町に伝わる民俗芸能の翁舞。
[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[車大歳神社の翁舞]]
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:奈良市|ならすひこしんしやのおきなまい]] [[category:日本の文化|ならすひこしんしやのおきなまい]] [[Category:伝統芸能|ならすひこしんしやのおきなまい]]

'''奈良豆比古神社の翁舞'''（ならずひこじんじゃのおきなまい）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]奈良坂町に伝わる民俗芸能の翁舞。
[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[車大歳神社の翁舞]]
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:奈良市|ならすひこしんしやのおきなまい]] [[Category:伝統芸能|ならすひこしんしやのおきなまい]]

revision.comment: ページ [[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]] を [[奈良豆比古神社]] へ移動: 神社の項目がないのに文化財だけあっても

'''奈良豆比古神社の翁舞'''（ならずひこじんじゃのおきなまい）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]奈良坂町に伝わる民俗芸能の翁舞。
[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[車大歳神社の翁舞]]

revision.comment: とりあえず神社の説明に
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=奈良豆比古神社| 所在地=奈良県奈良市奈良坂町奥垣内2489| 祭神=平城津彦神
施基親王
春日王| 社格=式内社（小）| 例祭=[[10月9日]]| 本殿=[[春日造]]}} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の論社の一社である。 ==祭神== 平城津彦神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、施基親王（[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、春日王（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ==祭事== 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される翁舞は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[車大歳神社の翁舞]] [[Category:奈良県の神社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良市|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:伝統芸能|ならつひこしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=奈良豆比古神社| 所在地=奈良県奈良市奈良坂町奥垣内2489| 祭神=平城津彦神
施基親王
春日王| 社格=式内社（小）| 例祭=[[10月9日]]| 本殿=[[春日造]]}} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の論社の一社である。 ==祭神== 平城津彦神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、施基親王（[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、春日王（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ==祭事== 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 関連項目 == *[[車大歳神社]]（[[車大歳神社の翁舞|翁舞]]） [[Category:奈良県の神社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良市|ならつひこしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=奈良豆比古神社| 所在地=奈良県奈良市奈良坂町奥垣内2489| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|16|N|135|50|03|E|}}| 祭神=平城津比古大神
春日宮天皇
春日王| 社格=式内社（小）| 創建=宝亀2年（771年）| 例祭=[[10月9日]]| 本殿=[[春日造]]}} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の楢神社を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に春日王（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、光仁天皇の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 ==祭事== 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 [[Category:奈良県の神社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|ならつひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良市|ならつひこしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 奈良豆比古神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市奈良坂町奥垣内2489 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|16|N|135|50|03|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 平城津比古大神
春日宮天皇
春日王 |社格 = 式内社（小）・旧村社 |創建 = 宝亀2年（771年） |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = }} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の楢神社を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に春日王（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 ==祭事== 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

八幡社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = }} {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]（[[奈良きたまち]]）にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。春日社とも八幡社とも称する。{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|page=|isbn=4-582-13402-5}}[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の[[楢神社]]を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に春日王（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 ==祭事== 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 注 == {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良市]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 奈良豆比古神社 |画像 = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市奈良坂町奥垣内2489 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|16|N|135|50|03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 平城津比古大神
春日宮天皇
春日王 |社格 = 式内社（小）・旧村社 |創建 = 宝亀2年（771年） |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = 春日社
八幡社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = }} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]（[[奈良きたまち]]）にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。春日社とも八幡社とも称する。{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|page=|isbn=4-582-13402-5}}[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の[[楢神社]]を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に[[春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|春日王]]（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 境内に、かつて「佐保神石」と呼ばれる神石があった{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=[[豊住書店]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}}。 当社の20年毎の御造替は戦中も欠かさず[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]によって行われ、「奈良阪の氏神さん」と呼ばれて敬愛されている当神社現地配布案内パンフレット。 ==古木== ===樟の巨樹=== 本殿裏境内地に[[クス|樟]]の巨樹が自生する。 奈良県の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている（[[1951年]]([[昭和]]26年)11月1日、[[1977年]](同52年)5月20日指定）。 1200年前の、「田原天皇の子、田原太子(春日王)が療養のため、大木茂る平城山の一社(当社)に隠居さる」との伝説を裏付けている。 樹齢1000年余、土際の幹囲約12.8m、目通りの幹囲約7.5m、樹高約30m、枝張り約20m当神社現地案内板 奈良県教育委員会。 地上約7mで南北に2分岐し、更に北枝幹は3m、南枝幹は4mのところで更に2分岐する。 ===児の手柏の切り株=== 境内に巨大な[[コノテガシワ|児の手柏]](万葉の樹)の切り株がある。 昭和27年([[1952年]])枯れたが、[[小清水卓二]]によると樹齢1300年にもなろうと評された。 ==祭事== * [[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]] - 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される。[[2000年]]([[平成]]12年)[[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

春日宮天皇
春日王 |社格 = 式内社（小）・旧村社 |創建 = 宝亀2年（771年） |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = 春日社
八幡社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 42|緯度秒 = 16 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 03 |地図 = Nara city }} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]（[[奈良きたまち]]）にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。春日社とも八幡社とも称する。{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|page=|isbn=4-582-13402-5}}[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の[[楢神社]]を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に[[春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|春日王]]（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 境内に、かつて「佐保神石」と呼ばれる神石があった{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=[[豊住書店]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}}。 当社の20年毎の御造替は戦中も欠かさず[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]によって行われ、「奈良阪の氏神さん」と呼ばれて敬愛されている当神社現地配布案内パンフレット。 ==古木== ===樟の巨樹=== 本殿裏境内地に[[クス|樟]]の巨樹が自生する。 奈良県の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている（[[1951年]]([[昭和]]26年)11月1日、[[1977年]](同52年)5月20日指定）。 1200年前の、「田原天皇の子、田原太子(春日王)が療養のため、大木茂る平城山の一社(当社)に隠居さる」との伝説を裏付けている。 樹齢1000年余、土際の幹囲約12.8m、目通りの幹囲約7.5m、樹高約30m、枝張り約20m当神社現地案内板 奈良県教育委員会。 地上約7mで南北に2分岐し、更に北枝幹は3m、南枝幹は4mのところで更に2分岐する。 ===児の手柏の切り株=== 境内に巨大な[[コノテガシワ|児の手柏]](万葉の樹)の切り株がある。 昭和27年([[1952年]])枯れたが、[[小清水卓二]]によると樹齢1300年にもなろうと評された。 ==祭事== * [[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]] - 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される。[[2000年]]([[平成]]12年)[[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|二の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|樟の巨木 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|手水舎 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|拝殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|本殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|摂社 辨財天 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|摂社 毘沙門天王 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|摂社 恵比須 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|摂社 石瓶神 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|摂社 福の神 }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

隣接する寺院。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]） |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = 春日社
八幡社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 42|緯度秒 = 16 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 03 |地図 = Nara city }} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]（[[奈良きたまち]]）にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。春日社とも八幡社とも称する。{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|page=|isbn=4-582-13402-5}}[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の[[楢神社]]を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に[[春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|春日王]]（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（天押雲根命）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 境内に、かつて「佐保神石」と呼ばれる神石があった{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=[[豊住書店]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}}。 当社の20年毎の御造替は戦中も欠かさず[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]によって行われ、「奈良阪の氏神さん」と呼ばれて敬愛されている当神社現地配布案内パンフレット。 ==古木== ===樟の巨樹=== 本殿裏境内地に[[クス|樟]]の巨樹が自生する。 奈良県の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている（[[1951年]]([[昭和]]26年)11月1日、[[1977年]](同52年)5月20日指定）。 1200年前の、「田原天皇の子、田原太子(春日王)が療養のため、大木茂る平城山の一社(当社)に隠居さる」との伝説を裏付けている。 樹齢1000年余、土際の幹囲約12.8m、目通りの幹囲約7.5m、樹高約30m、枝張り約20m当神社現地案内板 奈良県教育委員会。 地上約7mで南北に2分岐し、更に北枝幹は3m、南枝幹は4mのところで更に2分岐する。 ===児の手柏の切り株=== 境内に巨大な[[コノテガシワ|児の手柏]](万葉の樹)の切り株がある。 昭和27年([[1952年]])枯れたが、[[小清水卓二]]によると樹齢1300年にもなろうと評された。 ==祭事== * [[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]] - 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される。[[2000年]]([[平成]]12年)[[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 ==文化財== *'''（2020年度重要文化財指定予定）'''木造能面癋見（べしみ） 千種（ちぐさ）作 応永二十年二月、千草左衛門大夫作の刻銘がある{{Cite web |url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-03-20}}（官報告示を経て正式指定となる） == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|二の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|樟の巨木 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|手水舎 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|拝殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|本殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|摂社 辨財天 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|摂社 毘沙門天王 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|摂社 恵比須 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|摂社 石瓶神 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|摂社 福の神 }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]] * [[奈良きたまち]] * [[西福寺 (奈良市)|西福寺]] - 隣接する寺院。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

春日王 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]） |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = 春日社
八幡社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 42|緯度秒 = 16 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 03 |地図 = Nara city }} '''奈良豆比古神社'''（ならつひこじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]（[[奈良きたまち]]）にある[[神社]]である。かつては'''奈良坂春日社'''と称していた。春日社とも八幡社とも称する。{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|page=|isbn=4-582-13402-5}}[[式内社]]「大和国添上郡 奈良豆比古神社」の後裔社である（天理市の[[楢神社]]を奈良豆比古神社の後裔社とする説もあるが、あまり有力視されていない）。 ==祭神== 中殿に平城津比古大神（当地の[[産土神]]。奈良豆比古神とも）、左殿に春日宮天皇（施基親王、[[志貴皇子]]、田原天皇とも）、右殿に[[春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|春日王]]（志貴皇子の子）を祀る。 ただし、『式内社考』では中殿が南良春日宮大神（奈良豆比古神）、左殿が[[春日大社|春日]]若宮（[[天押雲根命]]）、右殿が矢幡大神（施基親王）としている。 ==歴史== 当地は、[[光仁天皇]]の父の施基親王（春日宮天皇）が病気療養のために隠居していた奈良山春日離宮の地であり、[[宝亀]]2年（[[771年]]）、その地に施基親王を祀ったのに始まる。 境内に、かつて「佐保神石」と呼ばれる神石があった{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=[[豊住書店]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}}。 当社の20年毎の御造替は戦中も欠かさず[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]によって行われ、「奈良阪の氏神さん」と呼ばれて敬愛されている当神社現地配布案内パンフレット。 ==古木== ===樟の巨樹=== 本殿裏境内地に[[クス|樟]]の巨樹が自生する。 奈良県の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている（[[1951年]]([[昭和]]26年)11月1日、[[1977年]](同52年)5月20日指定）。 1200年前の、「田原天皇の子、田原太子(春日王)が療養のため、大木茂る平城山の一社(当社)に隠居さる」との伝説を裏付けている。 樹齢1000年余、土際の幹囲約12.8m、目通りの幹囲約7.5m、樹高約30m、枝張り約20m当神社現地案内板 奈良県教育委員会。 地上約7mで南北に2分岐し、更に北枝幹は3m、南枝幹は4mのところで更に2分岐する。 ===児の手柏の切り株=== 境内に巨大な[[コノテガシワ|児の手柏]](万葉の樹)の切り株がある。 昭和27年([[1952年]])枯れたが、[[小清水卓二]]によると樹齢1300年にもなろうと評された。 ==祭事== * [[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]] - 毎年[[10月8日]]夜、秋の例祭の宵宮に町内の翁講中によって奉納される。[[2000年]]([[平成]]12年)[[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 ==文化財== ===重要文化財=== *木造能面癋見（べしみ） 千種（ちぐさ）作 応永二十年二月、千草左衛門大夫作の刻銘がある{{Cite web |url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-03-20}}令和2年9月30日文部科学省告示第118号 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|二の鳥居 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|樟の巨木 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|手水舎 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|拝殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|本殿 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|摂社 辨財天 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|摂社 毘沙門天王 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|摂社 恵比須 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|摂社 石瓶神 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|摂社 福の神 }} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]] * [[奈良きたまち]] * [[西福寺 (奈良市)|西福寺]] - 隣接する寺院。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならつひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Naratsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Shrine |Name = Naratsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[category:most recent 500]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - {{Shrine |Name = Naratsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Shrine |Name = Naratsuhiko Shrine |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami

Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Oldest 50 pages as of February 16 2025]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]]

However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]][[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=draft}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=draft}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子|12=draft}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=draft}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考|12=draft}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=draft}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=draft}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=draft}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=draft}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]
