Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=draft}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子|qq=draft}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社考|qq=draft}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子|qq=draft}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinai-sha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Village shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusuna|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusuna|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Shiki no Ōji|ja|志貴皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki no Ōji|qq=}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Naratsuhiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]]

Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = [[Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Naratsuhiko Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naratsuhiko jinja 20141002.jpg|300px]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Nara City]], Narazaka Town 2489 |Deities = Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami
Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 2 (771) |Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Narazaka Town, [[Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]

It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|qq=}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]], also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called [[:en:Prince Shiki|Shiki no Ōji]], or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|qq=}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|qq=}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E8%B1%86%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良市の Important Cultural Properties]] {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]]
