Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.704444|N|135.834167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kasugau-ō|WD=Q17277321|1=Kasugaō}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Narazaka Town, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|1=Nara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541342}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Shiki no Ōji|1=Prince Shiki}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|1=Kasugaō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17277321}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] by {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Platycladus orientalis|WD=Q33482}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|1=Kitamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444977}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|1=Saifuku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629350}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11445443}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|1=Nara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541342}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Shiki no Ōji|1=Prince Shiki}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|1=Kasugaō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17277321}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] by {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Platycladus orientalis|WD=Q33482}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|1=Kitamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444977}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|1=Saifuku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629350}} - Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.704444|N|135.834167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kasugau-ō|WD=Q17277321|1=Kasugaō}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Narazaka Town, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|1=Nara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541342}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Shiki no Ōji|1=Prince Shiki}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|1=Kasugaō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17277321}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] by {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Platycladus orientalis|WD=Q33482}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|1=Kitamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444977}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|1=Saifuku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629350}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11445443}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|1=Nara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541342}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Shiki no Ōji|1=Prince Shiki}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|1=Kasugaō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17277321}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] by {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Platycladus orientalis|WD=Q33482}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|1=Kitamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444977}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|1=Saifuku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629350}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} [[ja:奈良豆比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11445443}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō
Kasuga-Ō |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) |Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alias = Kasuga Shrine
Hachiman Shrine |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude = 34.7044 |Longitude = 135.8342 |Map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.704444|N|135.834167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kasugau-ō|WD=Q17277321|1=Kasugaō}}; {{ill|Shiki no miko|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} '''Naratsuhiko Shrine''' (奈良豆比古神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Narazaka Town, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was previously known as '''Narazaka Kasuga Shrine'''. It is also referred to as Kasuga Shrine or Hachiman Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|title=大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典|editor=平凡社地方資料センター|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1997|isbn=4-582-13402-5}} The shrine is considered the descendant of the {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Naratsuhiko Shrine in Sōekami District, Yamato Province" (though some theories suggest that {{ill|Nara Shrine (Tenri)|ja|楢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Shrine (Tenri)|1=Nara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541342}} in Tenri City may be its descendant, this view is less widely supported). == Enshrined Deities == The central hall enshrines Heijōtsuhiko no Ōkami (the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, also known as Naratsuhiko no Kami). The left hall enshrines Kasuga-no-Miya Tennō (Prince Shiki, also called {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|WD=Q11072254|lt=Shiki no Ōji|1=Prince Shiki}}, or Emperor Tahara), and the right hall enshrines {{ill|Kasuga-Ō|ja|春日王 (志貴皇子の子)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-Ō|1=Kasugaō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17277321}} (son of Prince Shiki). However, in the {{ill|Shikinaisha Kō|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Kō|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, it is stated that the central hall enshrines Minami-Nara Kasuga-no-Miya Ōkami (Naratsuhiko no Kami), the left hall enshrines {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}, and the right hall enshrines Yahata no Ōkami (Prince Shiki). == History == The shrine is located on the site of Kasuga Detached Palace in Narayama, where Prince Shiki (later known as Emperor Kasuga) retired for recuperation. It was established in {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 2 (771) to enshrine Prince Shiki. In the past, the shrine grounds contained a sacred stone called "Saho Shinseki" (佐保神石).{{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 正編|editor=[[Yamada Kumao]]|publisher=[[Toyozumi Shoten]]|year=1976|page=134|isbn=4-88617-005-6}} The shrine has maintained its tradition of being rebuilt every 20 years, even during wartime, through the devotion of local {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. It is affectionately known as "Narasaka no Ujigami-san."Pamphlet distributed on-site at the shrine. == Ancient Trees == === Giant Camphor Tree === A massive camphor tree grows naturally behind the main hall. It has been designated a [[:en:Natural Monument|Natural Monument]] by {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} since November 1, 1951, and May 20, 1977. The tree is believed to support the legend of "Prince Tahara, the son of Emperor Tahara, recovering at this shrine in the dense forest of Heijōyama." It is over 1,000 years old, with a trunk circumference of approximately 12.8 meters at the base, 7.5 meters at chest height, a height of about 30 meters, and a canopy spread of 20 meters.On-site information board by the Nara Prefecture Board of Education. === Stump of Child's Hand Cedar === The stump of a giant {{ill|Konotegashiwa|ja|コノテガシワ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konotegashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Platycladus orientalis|WD=Q33482}} (Child's Hand Cedar) remains in the shrine grounds. Although it died in 1952, scholar [[Koshimizu Takuji]] estimated its age at 1,300 years. == Festivals == * **Okinamai** - The {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} is held annually on the evening of October 8 during the shrine's autumn festival. It was designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on December 27, 2000. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Wooden Noh mask (Beshimi), inscribed with "Made by Chigusa Saemon-no-Daibu in February of Ōei 20" (1413).{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92093401.html|title=文化審議会答申 ～国宝・重要文化財（美術工芸品）の指定及び登録有形文化財（美術工芸品）の登録について～|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2020-03-20}}Notice by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology, September 30, 2020. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=150 |height=170 |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-1Torii.jpg|First Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-2Torii.jpg|Second Torii |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Kusu.jpg|Giant Camphor Tree |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Chozu.jpg|Chōzuya |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Honden.jpg|Main Hall |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Benten.jpg|Sessha Benzaiten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Bishamon.jpg|Sessha Bishamonten |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ebisu.jpg|Sessha Ebisu |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Ishigame.jpg|Sessha Ishikame no Kami |File:Naratsuhiko-Jinja-Fukunokami.jpg|Sessha Fukuno Kami }} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|ja|奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naratsuhiko Shrine Okinamai|1=Okina-mai of Naratsuhiko-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445444}} * {{ill|Nara Kitamachi|ja|奈良きたまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kitamachi|1=Kitamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11444977}} * {{ill|Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|ja|西福寺 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saifuku-ji (Nara City)|1=Saifuku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629350}} - Adjacent temple. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{osm box|w|398754703}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|奈良豆比古神社|version=97989389|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Naratsuhiko-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11445443|co|commons:Category:Naratsuhiko-jinja|ja|奈良豆比古神社}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
